title: tanacetum argenteum subsp. argenteum
intro: Tanacetum argenteum subsp. argenteum, also known as Silver Tansy or White Tansy, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Tanacetum argenteum subsp. argenteum include its potential to reduce fever, ease digestive issues, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or using a solvent such as ethanol.
